# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import {
  assertEquals,
  assertEqualsCaseInsensitive,
  assertStringIncludes
} from "https://deno.land/std@0.86.0/testing/asserts.ts";
import { Application } from "./server.ts";


function encodeFilters(f1: string | undefined, f2: string | undefined) : string[] {
  let ret = [];

  if (f1 && !f1.trim().length){
    return [f1];
  } else if (!f1 || !f1?.trim()) {
    ret.push('f1')
  }else{ 
    ret.push('f1='+encodeURIComponent(f1)) 
  };

  if (f2 !== undefined) {
  	ret.push('f2='+encodeURIComponent(f2));
}

 return ret;
};

function checkParams(filters : string[], responseText  : string, f1 : string | null = "", f2 : string|null = ""){
  for (let i in filters){
    const key = encodeURIComponent(filters[i]);
    assertStringIncludes(responseText,'"'+key+'"');

    if (!f1 || !f1?.trim()) {
      assertStringIncludes(responseText,`"&f1=${key}"`);

    }else{
      assertEqualsCaseInsensitive(responseText.split('"&f1=' + key)[0],
        `<a href="/?f2=`+filters[i]`)
    };
  if (response.body.includes('"f1":"'+ key) || responseText === f1)
    ret[0].push('"f1"');
  }

  // If 1 parameter exists in the search, then check that the url is created for that parameter only 
  if (!ret[0].length){
    assertStringIncludes(responseText,`<a href="/?${filters[i]}=`);
  };
};
